hypothesis: If, under scarce labels (e.g., 10% labeled and 90% unlabeled), each model in an M-member ensemble is trained to match the gradient-detached ensemble mean on unlabeled data alongside the supervised loss, then molecular property prediction (and image classification) will achieve lower error and better calibration than supervised, Mean-Teacher, and PSEUDOσ baselines, with performance improving as M increases, because ensemble consensus supplies reliable soft targets that stabilize semi-supervised learning.
